entry.title: cash flow analyser and confirmed quantity in sales order
entry.content:
dears,

we are implementing cash flow analyser and realised that only confirmed quantity in sales order is taken in consideration. 

we produced "machinery", so when we open a sales order (even if the customer confirmed the purchase) the quantity is not yet available in stock. how can we include these NOT yet CONFIRMED quantities in the cash flow analaser report?

does anybody have similar problem? 

thanks

entry.title: SFTP Time Events
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We're currently facing an issue with the replication of time events via SFTP to SuccessFactors Time Tracking. The clock-in/clock-out records are being sent correctly through the SFTP files, and the time type event is properly defined in the files.

However, in some cases, when the data is imported into SuccessFactors, the wrong time type event gets associated with the entry. This doesn't happen consistently — only with specific records, while others are processed correctly.

Has anyone experienced something similar or have any idea what might be causing this inconsistent behavior? Is there any known issue with the SFTP import mechanism that could lead to this?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: inbound delivery released after packing not appearing in extended
entry.content:
Inbound delivery was created after packing and saved message received that delivery distributed in Extended EWM System.

Message received in SLG1 as below

But this delivery could not be traced in neither /SCWM/PRDI nor in SMQ2 or SMQ1, But when Inbound delivery got created without packing same was duly found in /SCWM/PRDI.

Could any one provide any clue.

Regards

Indrajit

entry.title: Re: Subledger Source Document Type in Journal Entry Query
entry.content:
Hi @kedalenechong ,

Not sure if this is what you meant exactly, but:

OJDT.TransType: Specifies the type of transaction that generated the journal entry, such as an A/P invoice, or any other marketing document/payment. Link to the object type (check the list here: List of object types - SAP Business One - SAP Community).

OJDT.BaseRef: Contains the document number or other reference from the original business document (e.g., an invoice number). This, along with the TransType, helps identify the source document.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Limited Time Activation of virtual Super-User - SAP*
entry.content:
SAP Note 3303172

Time-limited client-specific activation of the virtual super-user SAP*, available as of kernel release 790:

  1. Logon to the operating system of an application server using <sid>adm user.
  2. Start the interactive tool dpmon with menu option u to activate the virtual client-specific super-user SAP* in a chosen client. Define a period of between 10 and 30 minutes as validity. You will obtain a one-time password after successful activation.
  3. Logon using user SAP* and the one-time password you have obtained on any currently running application server.

Point to be noted :

  • Any password based logon attempt for user SAP*, regardless if successful or not, invalidates the one-time password immediately.
  • You can (re-)activate the user using dpmon any time and will obtain a new one-time password. This is needed for example if you had a typo in your attempt to use the one-time password and could not logon.
  • Within dpmon you have further the possibility to see in which clients a virtual super-user does currently exist, which one still has a one-time password, their remaining existence and you are able to delete them prematurely.
  • An existing user SAP* (and an emergency super-user SAP* activated by the second option) is superimposed by an existing virtual super-user SAP* in a client.
  • A maximum of 20 virtual super-user SAP* can exist in parallel in different clients.
  • There is no need for an application server restart as no static profile parameter needs to be changed.
  • The user has no hardcoded known password but gets a new random one-time password after each activation.
  • The user is activated client-specific.
  • The user existence is limited in time, its validity is chosen during its activation within dpmon(the allowed period is between 10 and 30 minutes), and the user can also be deleted within dpmon already before its expiration. Bear in mind: a user deletion has no influence on an already established session. A session does continue to run.
  • Both activation and usage of the virtual super-user are logged in the security audit log event EUP (purpose 2).

 

Security Audit Log for virtual user SAP*

entry.title: Re: Ex Subj : My requirement is Excel file upload in Buil...
entry.content:
To upload an Excel file in SAP Build Apps and create a record, you can follow these steps:

Steps to Upload and Process Excel File

  1. File Upload Component:

    • Use a file upload component to allow users to select and upload the Excel file. This component can be added to your SAP Build Apps project.

  2. Backend Processing:

    • Once the file is uploaded, you need to process the file in the backend. You can use a service action to handle the file upload and parsing.
    • For parsing the Excel file, you can use libraries such as xlsx in Node.js or Java. This will help you read the content of the Excel file and convert it into a format that can be processed.

  3. Creating Records:

    • After parsing the Excel file, you can create records in your database. You can use batch inserts to efficiently handle large amounts of data.
    • Implement an optimized procedure for mass operations to avoid performance issues. Here is an example of how you can define and handle such an action in CAP (Cloud Application Programming):
service MyService {
  action massOperations(x: YourParameters) returns String;
}

srv.on('massOperations', async req => {
  // implement optimized procedure
  return "Success";
});

To move forward, you’ll might need to switch to a Q system where DRC configuration is allowed

Let me know if you still need further information

Thanks and kind regards

Chuma

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP Community!
entry.content: Welcome @Holk_H - I started SAP the same year as you, but in Ireland in the Galway office! Which part of Germany are you based in? We have some German location groups already set up by community members - in case you are interested to join any local events. There are often local SAP CodeJams too.

entry.title: Re: Subject : My requirement is Excel file upload in Buil...
entry.content:
Hello Mohit, thanks for reaching ou to us, Are you still encountering an issue ? if yes you can follow these steps: to upload an Excel file in SAP Build Apps and create a new record upon submission, 

Create a File Upload Component:

Add a file upload component to your page where users can select and upload their Excel files.
Handle File Upload:

Use the FileReader API to read the content of the uploaded Excel file. You can parse the file using a third-party library like xlsx from npm

Parse Excel Data:

Parse the Excel file data to extract the necessary information. This can be done using the xlsx package to convert the file content into a JSON format.
Create OData Batch Request:

Create an OData batch request to push the parsed data to the backend. This involves creating a service action in your CAP model to handle mass operations
service MyService {
action massOperations(x: YourParameters) returns String;
}

srv.on('massOperations', async req => {
// implement optimized procedure
return "Success";
});
Backend Processing:

Implement the backend logic to validate and insert/update the data into SAP HANA. You can refer to the example provided by Thomas Jung for mass operations
Submit Data:

Upon submission, ensure the data is sent to the backend service for processing. Handle any errors or validation issues appropriately.

entry.title: EasyDMS Question related to Q system refresh from P
entry.content:
We refreshed our ECQ system from ECP last month and now we cannot run any WWI reports out of our Q system since we didnt copy EasyDMS doc from P to Q.

We would like to copy documents from EasyDMS P to EasyDMS Q for about 500-1000 Specs/recipes (materials). 

Is there a way to do this and what are the steps. I am new to PLM and no one knows in basis team what to do.

Any help you can provide would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Getting error while installing Desktop Agent 3
entry.content:
Hello Jagadhatri, Are you still encountering an issue? please follow below steps to Install the desktop Agent 

    • For all users:
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Ensure the agent is installed in the folder %programfiles%\SAP\SAPDesktopAgent 
    • For the current user:
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Open the downloaded SAP Desktop Agent Service Setup file and follow the setup wizard instructions.
      • Ensure the files are installed in the folder %localappdata%\Programs\SAPDesktopAgent 

  2. Uninstall and Reinstall:

    • If switching from all users mode to per current user mode, uninstall the previous installation first before reinstalling

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Perú - SAP...
entry.content:
Hi @Jeremy_Deo

thank you very much for your support and time

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Eliminate EC data freeze period during compensation p...
entry.content:
@vinkamath Here you go (from the 2018 version of the official kickoff deck - now removed).

 

 

Option 1 – STATIC

Option 2 – DYNAMIC

How it works:

Demographic data like title, salary, & Pay Grade is frozen during cycle by disabling file feeds.

Nightly SFTP process or integration remains enabled. Changes to job, salary, etc. are made during cycle.

Pros

1)Easier to configure and test

2)Suited to small organizations, those with very short planning windows and who expect very few data elements to change

1)Suited to large organizations, long planning windows, or those where a large number of changes are expects

2)Data updated in the HRMS is automatically accommodated for in the planning worksheets

Cons / Considerations

1)Could negatively impact other impact modules like Performance and Recruiting which need daily updates to data.

2)Need to manually address termination/transfers/data impacting eligibility

3)Will require reconciliation of any updated data after the conclusion of the process

4)System will not respond to data updates, mimicking a point in time spreadsheet

5)May not be valid for certain integration types such as EC integrated templates or those using certain APIs

1)More difficult during implementation to set up to confirm all data updates and responds to changes as intended

2)May be a cultural adjustment as things such as budget not always static

3)Does not accommodate certain items where a business process requires frozen data, such as a point in time budget.

*Note that the manager hierarchy can be separated from this process depending on hierarchy type. This will be discussed in the following section.

entry.title: Re: SCM | org chart Key Position indicator permission res...
entry.content:
Hi @pboulicaut,

The first thing to make note of is your nomination method, as the behaviour is different depending on which one you use.

KBA: 2569082 - How to identify the Sucession Nomination Method Configured for Your Instance

MDF Position Nomination Method -

This is the nomination method which I suspect you are using based on the details included in your post. 

The "Key Position" field and icon on the Succession Org Chart is linked to the positionCriticality field in in the MDF Position Object. 

You can check the label, visibility (editable, read only or not visible) and the status of the field (active or inactive) by the following this path way;

Configure Object Definitions > Select "Object Definitions" and "Position" > Find the positionCriticality field > Select Details > Review label, visibility and status fields

From here you will see the label of field as it may be renamed in your system, the visibility of field may be set to not visible, or the status is sent to not active, which all may be reasons why you don't see the field at field level overrides level. 

If you can identify the reason from the suggestions above, then you can continue to follow the recommendations in KBA 2613393 by setting the field level overrides to read only or not visible according to your business requirements.

Legacy Position Nomination Method - 

I am including this for transparency or for anyone else interested in this topic

This nomination method is not based on the metadata framework (MDF) then the access to the "Key Position" field is not handled by field level overrides. 

The "Key Position" field and icon are if position’s incumbent is within the target population of Succession Management and Matrix Report permission or Manage User permission or Succession Planning permission of the logged in user. 

Further information can be seen in KBA 2679848 on the launchpad. 

Additional Information:

Admin Guide: Configuring Key Position Indicator
Wiki: Succession Planning - Restricting Target Population for the Position Object

I hope this helps with your query and if there is anything which needs clarification feel free to let me know.

Best Regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA 2022 FPS1 Fully-Activated Appliance standard installation
entry.content:
S/4HANA 2023 Fully-Activated Appliance standard installation Disp+Work Stop

no connection data found for key (DEFAULT) in HANA secure store
C connect via secure store failed, rc = -10104 (Invalid value for KEY "DEFAULT", directory search path: /home/s4hadm/.hdb/sid-hdb-s4h)
C *** ERROR => Connect to database failed, rc=-10909, rcSQL=-10104
[dbhdbsql.cpp 630]
C SQLCODE : -10104
C SQLERRTEXT : Invalid value for KEY "DEFAULT", directory search path: /home/s4hadm/.hdb/sid-hdb-s4h
B ***LOG BY2=> sql error -10104 performing CON [dbsh 1329]
B ***LOG BY0=> Invalid value for KEY "DEFAULT", directory search path: /home/s4hadm/.hdb/sid-hdb-s4h [dbsh 1329]
M ***LOG R19=> ThDbConnect, db_connect () [thxxtool.c 6114]
M ThIErrHandle: in_ThErrHandle: 1
M *** ERROR => ThInit: db_connect (step TH_INIT, thRc ERROR-DB-CONNECT_ERROR, action STOP_WP, level 1) [thxxhead.c 2851]
M

entry.title: Re: CAMT.053 and CAMT.052 in SAP ECC
entry.content: yes but MT942 will be discontinued (SWIFT plans to discontinue the use of the MT standard for statements and reports in MT94X format and for payment requests in MT101 format in November 2025, replacing it with camt.053/camt.052 for statements and reports and pain.001m for payment requests. SWIFT will continue to support the MT standard until November 2025, after which it will be replaced by ISO 20022 and the new camt.053/camt.052/pain.001 formats will become mandatory for all SWIFT members.)

entry.title: Re: Multi VAT on single invoice line

Also, we were still not able to select different tax codes on split tax lines.

entry.title: Re: Access Performance Forms Directly from SAP SuccessFactors Stories: New in 1H 2025!

entry.title: Re: Password reset emails - external candidates
entry.content: Hi all,

entry.title: Re: Multi VAT on single invoice line
entry.content: @Prem_Tracz, Is it possible to connect with you directly to talk more about this issue? Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Adding Successors Column to Calibration Session
entry.content:
Hi @Uroojafz,

This is not something which is currently possible in the system and the Talent Card would be the tool used in this scenario.

If adding, editing or removing successors for a specific group in a meeting environment (such as is often the case for Calibration or Talent Review meetings). Then perhaps looking at the presentations tool would be something of interest. You can configure a Talent Card like you can in Calibration, and engage in succession planning through the nomination portlet on the Talent Card. 

Admin Guide: Presentations

I hope I answered your query clearly, however if anything is unclear feel free to revert back.

Best Regards,

Michael

entry.title: Re: Password reset emails - external candidates
entry.content: Hi all, same issue here. Since it doesn't have an easy solution. Is there some kind of an audit trail to make sure that the emails have been sent from ssff? That way we can assure the client that the issue is not being caused by ssff

entry.title: Re: Hide People selector button in Goals. release 2025
entry.content: Hi, Would it be possible to disable the "Hide People Selector" button or, alternatively, customize its translation? I'm asking because our platform is mostly used in Spanish and Portuguese, and it's very likely that users will start reporting this button.

entry.title: Re: Enable faster user changes and shared terminals using badges in SAP Digital Manufacturing
entry.content:
Hi @ManoelCosta,

I have some questions about the (intended) scope of the badge access

  1. Is it just meant to be a "proof" of being physically there (e.g. on a assembly line) and does not tie in with authentication and authorization whatsoever
  2. Is it meant to elaborate the actual authorizations to another level, so e.g. for a specific time the logged on user gains more authorizations within DM?
  3. Is (could) it even be used (misused rather?) to authenticate (not authorize) a user towards DM, so by presenting the badge one would be logged onto the DM system as a named user representing that badge

My assumption is 1. but I'm curious about 3 also, even If I have doubts to go down that route, should that be even technically feasible.

Many thanks

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: Re: Successfactors initial deployment - Identity provisio...
entry.content: Many thanks, we opted for the (most probably) necessary (because of customAttribute1) solution to have the user store in Cloud Identity Services. One would also need the user store there for future technologies like Task Center or Jule

entry.title: Re: Query Input Parameter does not show in AfO Excel, RSR...
entry.content:
The solution is most likely (We used S4 Embeddd BW):
Due to a recent  ( 05.2025) security update its possible that an additional Authorisation Object
 needs to be added to Your roles:

AfO: Authorization Checks 
The SU01 / PFCG Authorization Object to b added is S_RS_AO.

The parameters are:
Authorizations for the SAP NetWeaver platform

entry.title: Re: FIORI: SAP Standard tiles not translated
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm facing a similar issue.

I have customized catalogs with TILES from standard catalogs.

The customization was done in EN and when I LOGED in FR, the TITLE TILES don't change, all the content inside the TILE change, but the TITLE no. 

I have clear the caches and checked SM59 that has no language set.

I have also checked that if I want to change my catalogs when LOGED in FR it is impossible.

You have any suggestion ?

entry.title: Re: SAP ALM API & Integration: Tasks/Defects/Requirements to Jira
entry.content:
Hello,

We have the same questions as mentioned at the beginning of the comments,
We have connected our SAP Cloud ALM to Jira using Task to Jira Task API, which enables us to create,  update, and delete a task in Jira from CALM.
We'd now like a bi-directional connection to update the task in Jira and see the result reflected in CALM.
It seems we need to create an automation in Jira, but it's not clear how to go about it.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Filters in Integration Center for Onboarding - Empty data
entry.content: Hi, did you find a solution to this? we are facing the same issue.

entry.title: Hello GRC Community,
entry.content:
Hello GRC Community,

I'm Benita, and I'm excited to join this group and start engaging with fellow professionals who are passionate about SAP GRC. I’ve had the opportunity to gain some hands-on experience with access controls, audit management, and user provisioning in SAP environments. More recently, I’ve been exploring how AI and automation are starting to enhance compliance processes and improve risk detection.

As I continue to grow in this space, I’m looking to connect with others working across different areas of GRC.

    I’d love to hear from you:

  • What part of GRC are you currently involved in—access control, risk management, audit, compliance, or something else?

  • Are there any groups within the SAP community (or outside of it) that you’ve found especially helpful for learning or collaboration?

  • And how is AI showing up in your space—if at all?

Looking forward to the conversations, insights, and connections. Thank you for welcoming me into the community!

Warm regards,
Benita

entry.title: Re: How to send notification email to receiver in Flexibl...
entry.content: Thanks. you mean even using flexible WF, I still need to config IMap? It cannot trigger Email on Fiori like Situation handler?

entry.title: post array using ODataV4 / V2
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

 

I have this JSON Post in Odata 

{
"brand" : "ShopRite",
"fundingType" : 10,
"offerCode" : "25041101",
"gs1CoPrefix" : "",
"familyCode" : "0",
"offerSeries" : "1",
"manufacturerCodes" : [ "text", "text2" ],
"taxesReclaimable" : false,
"isTender" : false
}

manufacturerCodes is the array expected by the client.

How can we configure and implement a system to handle arrays in SEGW for OData? I know there are complex types or Collection types in V4, but those require a property (the sub-object has a field label like)  

"manufacturerCodes" : [  code: "text",

                                        code "text2" ]

or 

"manufacturerCodes" : [ { code: "text", }

                                       { code: "text2"  }

                                  ],

Any idea? Is there any standard approach to post a field with an array to SAP OData?

If we post, I'm having this error: "error while parsing an XML stream". Already tried using a collection or complex type.

 

Please
Thanks a lot for your time to help

AK

entry.title: Variable Pay: Business Goals Assignment to Bonus Plans
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I have the current use case for Variable Pay: my customer wants to limit the number of Bonus Plans he needs to configure across the group. The idea would be to have generic Bonus plans, e.g. engineering, management, etc, and then be able to assign business goals per entity - see below example.

Bonus PlanBusiness GoalGoal weight
Engineering (business20% + Individual80%)EBIT Germany50%
Engineering (business20% + Individual80%)Revenue Germany50%
Engineering (business20% + Individual80%)EBIT UK50%
Engineering (business20% + Individual80%)Revenue UK50%

Is this even possible? Is the system allowing this and can then do the proper assignment of the business goals to the employees based on the eligibility criteria within the business goal?

If this is not possible, is there another way in order to avoid to have the same Bonus Plan per entity due to the business goals assignment?

Any suggestions? @xavierlegarrec 
SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

Really appreciate any help on the topic.

Best regards,

Susana Ferreira

entry.title: How to create a customer Sales Order type in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
entry.content:
How to create a customer Sales Order type in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create a customer Sales Order type in S/4HANA...
entry.content:
• Currently, on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's not possible to create new sales order types or copy existing ones.
  • However, a feature request was already created and accepted for this business requirement.
  • At the moment, Customers can subscribe to the Early Adopter Program and test this functionality before it's released. Check more information here: SAP Early Adopter Care: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - Create Custom Sales Order Type by Copying Standard Sales Order Type Templates

entry.title: Re: ME21N - Message 06081 - different order unit than price unit
entry.content: Hi, this is price condition unit measure factor, and you can maintain the combination in VK11.

entry.title: Re: currency in comp information portlets
entry.content:
Hi @kameshedf 

I will let EC experts chime in here as this is a purely EC topic.

But off the top of my head and since I work closely on that part with EC experts as part of Comp implementations the first thing that comes to mind is that for payroll consistency purposes you would still load all pay components in the currency they should paid in and would simply use currency conversion rates and pay component group designs to make sure everything gets shown in one currency only at an aggregated level.

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Questionmark cannot launch sample exam / I s this the...
entry.content: I have also the issue, the system says, my Teams is open but it is closed. What can I do?

entry.title: Re: SAP Document AI — The Roadmap for Seamless Document Processing in SAP Business Suite
entry.content: I'm not able to access the video

entry.title: Re: Variable Pay: Business Goals Assignment to Bonus Plans
entry.content:
Hi @sferreira 

yes it is possible, it's actually the most standard design of variable pay and leading practice you can have. As long as you don't need different sections weightings (for example 80% business and 20% individual in one case but 50-50 in another case for different groups of engineers) then you don't need different Engineering plans.

Business goals assignment to the employee work based on the attribute and value you select when you create them (they must be tied through your admin tools mapping to the bonus plan assigned to the employee, but they have their own flexibility in being assigned by country or business unit or location or whichever other criteria).

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Create custom tab in XD02/XD03 transaction
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I need to add a custom tab in XD02/XD03 tcode .

A customer number is entered and F4 help is pressed.

In the next pop up screen, a custom tab has to be entered right at the end.

Can anyone help me with the interface and the method that I will need to implement this logic ?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Crystal Reports License Renewal
entry.content: The user Temp folder (%TEMP%) for Installation Package extraction is on a separate Network Drive with Limited Space. It causes the failure.

entry.title: Deprecated class de.hybris.platform.webservicescommons.util.YSanitizer
entry.content:
de.hybris.platform.webservicescommons.util.YSanitizer is marked as deprecated since 2211.23, but it doesn't say which class to use instead. There's de.hybris.platform.util.Sanitizer, but the method sanitize() seems to be a bit different. 

What should I replace de.hybris.platform.webservicescommons.util.YSanitize with?

Thank you!

entry.title: Individual multiplier schemes for each assignment - Variable Pay Calculation
entry.content:
My client has a requirement where for each of the assignments, individual multiplier scheme varies. For reference, this is a multiplicative scheme.

Ex: If for 1st six months employee was a Specialist, individual performance multiplier should be 200%. In mid of the year, employee got promoted to Manager level  and individual performance multiplier should be 400% for assignment 6 months since promotion. 

Individual Performance Multiplier is dependent on Grade Category (which is a combination of multiple fields like Job Level, Job Band, Employee Group and Employee Subgroup) and Rating. There will be only 1 PM form for entire year.

My approach has been to get the Individual Performance Multipler in EH file and use it in the Basis calculation, so that assignment target amount is already inclusive of the Individual Multiplier. And use Individual guidelines as 1 (catch all formula) for all cases. This seems to be working fine, however, my concern is that this Individual Performance Multiplier - how should this be stored in EC - in Job info? What would be the best possible solution which does not involve too much effort for client?

Any help/guidance is much appreciated. 

@xavierlegarrec

entry.title: Re: Learn how to configure SAP Build Process Automation tasks for SAP Task Center
entry.content:
Hi @Chaitanya_Priya ,

In SAP Mobile Start I cannot see the Approve/Reject buttons for tasks that came from SAP Build Process Automation, I need to open the form in the source inbox to be able to see them.

Is that a limitation for SBPA tasks?

Regards

Mauricio

entry.title: Re: Picklist field error_invalid number format
entry.content:
Hi @mithilapethe , hope you're doing good.

From the information you've shared, I suggest you 2 configurations to be checked:

  1. Apparently the field "State" is not properly configured as a picklist, and that's the reason this field is not behaving as such. Please compare the "State" field configuration with the "Country" field Configuration, since it's expected for these fields to have the same picklist configuration, except for the Valid source picklist value.
  2. If there's a parent-child relationship between Country and State, then maybe you can check how this can be configured in the following KBA: 2861689 - How to create MDF Picklist and set a parent-child relationship between MDF picklists?

Please let me know if above suggestions can help you with this issue, and in case they did not helped, then check if there's any other information that can be shared here so our community can check and investigate further.

Regards.

Thiago Eva

entry.title: A report or feasible view is needed to view or analyze Sales order line-item costing
entry.content:
Users are required to get the sales order line-item wise costing in a single report. Please suggest which SAP Fiori app can provide this functionality in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: A report or feasible view is needed to view or analyz...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the classic report "Display Actual Cost Line Items – Sales Orders (App ID: KVBI)" is not available as it is only supported in the On-Premise and Private Cloud editions.

For Public Cloud users, the best alternative to analyze costing at the sales order line-item level is the Fiori app “Sales Volume – Profit Margin” (App ID: F0996).

This app provides:

  • Line-item level visibility into sales volume, cost of goods sold (COGS), and profit margin.
  • Drill-down by sales organization, product, or customer.
  • Gross margin KPIs directly on the Fiori launchpad.

You can access the app in your system or view it in the SAP Fiori Apps Library.

If further granularity is needed, consider using the Custom Analytical Queries app to create a tailored report that includes sales order line-item cost and margin data.

entry.title: SBC in ePPDS with CDP : No Process Selection Criteria popup
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I’m currently activating aATP SBC along with ePPDS and characteristic dependent planning as documented in this blog post (https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-s-4hana-aatp-supply-creation-based-confirmation-sbc-series-part-4/ba-p/13565415).

According to the blog post and to some OSS notes I consulted, a popup called “Process Selection Criteria” should appear and ask for characteristics value for the batch determination. This is not happening and I have to manually trigger the batch determination that send me to the “usual old” SD determination screen:

If I modify the selection criteria in this screen, there are correctly taken into account into /SAPAPO/RRP3 to match with the expected batch, but this is not at all practical and this is misleading for users as no batch are selected in the sales order.

Steps already taken:

  • I have successfully activated the BC set switch /SAPAPO/APO_ON_ERP_SWITCHED
  • Configuration schema is set to CDP in SPRO
  • I have created a KMAT material with usual settings (300 class, profile, etc.) and configured a finished material in the plant data of the finished material.
  • I have created and assigned a class 023 to the finished material.
  • I have assigned organizational area to class and characteristics and created and activated integration models for these classes to create class 400 and 230
  • I have set up a checking group with PPAC check type and Supply Creation and assigned it to my finished material master
  • I have assigned a batch search strategy to sales order type and maintained required conditions records.

Any idea of why this is not working or where to debug this is welcome 🙂

entry.title: Re: Quantity Adjustment in SAP EWM using Process Codes
entry.content:
@former_member721115, 
Can you please help me with the code that worked for you for EWM delivery quantity update?

Regadrs

Felipe Cadena

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: Cannot allocate enough memory:Allocat...
entry.content: Perhaps you should check the memory in your DPAgent

entry.title: SAP Sapphire 2025: Notes from Day 1 Keynote with a focus on Developers
entry.content:
If you're a developer working in or around SAP, Sapphire can feel like drinking from a firehose of buzzwords. But if you zoom in a bit, this year had quite a bit that’s relevant if you build, extend, or operate on SAP.

This isn’t a recap of every announcement. Just the ones that I thought matter most to folks writing code, managing extensions, or figuring out how all this new AI stuff fits together. It was often the items that I found our team of Developer Advocates discussing in our back channel as we watched the Keynote together this morning.

AI Runtime, Prompt Optimization, and a Foundation for Builders

One of the more impactful announcements this year was the launch of the newly, updated AI Foundation. This isn’t a single tool, it’s a collection of services meant to help developers build, extend, and run AI more effectively across SAP. Personally, I'm looking forward to playing around with this soon. Maybe seeing it some of our upcoming CodeJams.

The most interesting piece of the AI Foundation might be the AI Agent Runtime in SAP BTP. It gives you a way to run AI agents natively, not as one-off scripts or sidecar tools. Alongside that, SAP introduced a Prompt Optimizer, which can take vague prompt ideas and turn them into tuned, testable input saving hours of trial and error. This also promises to make your prompts model agnostic, doing for AI what SAP has done for years when it comes to running applications on different operating systems.

Joule Studio, part of SAP Build, now supports skill and agent creation with low-code tools, hooks into SAP data, and context via the Knowledge Graph. You can bet we will see a whole more about Joule Studio later this year at SAP TechEd!

OSS Note 3437766: Availability of Generative AI Models

SAP Sapphire Innovation Guide 2025 | Business AI

Joule Agents: More Than a Copilot

Joule is evolving. This year, the story was about Joule Agents; modular, task-specific AI workers that will observe, reason, and act.

These agents are powered by SAP’s reasoning models, built on top of structured enterprise data, and connect across systems. They’re not stuck inside SAP thanks to Agent2Agent, a new protocol developed with Google, agents from different vendors can work together.

Full SAP Joule Agents announcement

Business Data Cloud: Structuring for AI at Scale

AI is only useful if it understands your data. That’s where SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) fits. It’s a harmonized foundation of curated, governed data built specifically to fuel AI-powered scenarios across SAP.

This year’s additions include:

  • Intelligent apps like People Intelligence and Spend Control Tower
  • A knowledge graph that gives agents and analytics the ability to understand data relationships
  • Availability across Google BigQuery, AWS, and Azure

Explore the full BDC announcement

Perplexity Partnership: Smarter Answers with a Wider Context

SAP and Perplexity AI are working together to bring a new kind of search-and-answer capability to Joule. The idea is to combine SAP's structured business data with trusted real-time external content to generate more complete, context-aware answers.

So, if you’re asking Joule about something happening within your business, you’re not limited to internal systems. Joule can pull in relevant external information from the web with full sourcing details.

Details on SAP + Perplexity partnership

SAP BTP: Better Tools, Cleaner Extensions

Plenty of updates to SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) this year, most of them developer-relevant:

  • SAP Build is getting more capable. Joule Studio lets you build agents. New tools help you extend apps with less wiring.
  • AI-assisted ABAP tooling helps you migrate ECC code and align with clean core expectations. (See recent videos on SAP Developers Channel for examples).
  • Integration Suite adds predictive monitoring for APIs and an AI adapter that lets you slot in your own LLMs.
  • And finally, BTP services will be available through hyperscaler marketplaces like AWS and GCP later this year.

Full BTP update details

Final Thoughts

Sapphire 2025 sets the stage for TechEd 2025, naturally. If you want to get your hands-on with many of the announcements from today, then already start planning to attend TechEd or one of the TechEd on Tour events.

Some of the things we can look forward to trying out soon:

  • An AI runtime with tooling
  • Agentic AI that spans systems
  • A data layer built for AI use, not just reporting
  • BTP enhancements that feel aligned with how developers work

If you’re working on AI extensions, modernizing ABAP, or trying to get your data story right; this is the time to get ready to learn!

entry.title: Re: How to Prepare for the move to SAP Global Trade Services, edition for SAP HANA in 5 Steps
entry.content:

Cheers

Jens

entry.title: How to create a new org unit without affecting the current set up in development customizing?
entry.content:
Knowing that after creating a organizational unit it cannot be deleted, would be possible to create a new organizational unit, like a company code, without affecting the current organizational unit?

For example, being able to test a new organization without affecting what is already set up in the development customizing tenant?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create a new org unit without affecting the cu...
entry.content:
Hello,

In a 3-system landscape this can be achieved using Parallel Line. The Parallel Line offers you a separate workspace in SAP Central Business Configuration and two additional tenants for configuration and testing in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. The main line focuses on maintaining innovation and keeping the productive environment up-to-date with SAP's latest features. With a separate parallel line, you can develop and test configuration enhancements without affecting the main line. The Parallel Line needs to be requested through " SAP for Me".

Please refer to Parallel Line in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition | SAP Help Portal 

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Archiving asset accounting - Difference between testr...
entry.content: I have the same issue, did anyone find a solution?

entry.title: Re: TransactionTimedoutException in sap cpi when using huge payload
entry.content: Hi. Any luck with the resolution for this issue?

entry.title: Re: Empowering business to manage Decisions in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content: Thanks @Sesh_Sreenivas. Much needed feature, esp for business users to maintain their decision/rule conditions in a user friendly way.

entry.title: Re: Public View in S/4Hana Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

To identify the business catalog in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud that allows you to save a view as public, you can utilize the SAP Fiori apps reference library. Here you can search and review the details associated with the apps and their respective business catalogs.

Steps to Find Business Catalog:

  1. Access SAP Fiori Apps Library: Go to SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library and select All apps for SAP S/4HANA Cloud under the Categories section.
  2. Search for App Name or ID: Identify the app related to saving views (e.g., ‘Manage Views’, ‘View Settings’, etc.) in the search area.
  3. Check Implementation Information: Within the app details, go to the IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION tab and expand the Configuration section where you will find the required business catalogs.

If you still face difficulties, consult the Maintain Business Roles app to verify which business catalog contains the specific functionalities required for saving a view as public.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How to Restrict WBS Element Usage in Sales Order Base...
entry.content:
Hello,

To address the need for restricting the assignment of WBS elements in the Sales Order creation process based on the project or project type in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, you can utilize the solution involving the use of custom logic in App Extensibility: WBS ElementBusiness Add-Ins (BAdIs).

Steps to Implement Custom Validation:

1. Utilize In-App Extensibility

SAP S/4HANA allows you to extend applications via In-App extensibility with custom logic. You can use the Custom Logic app to create validations.

2. Use Business Add-Ins (BAdIs)

  • Implement custom logic using the BAdIs available for Project Elements.
  • Two relevant BAdIs are available:
    • Project Element Validation - Enterprise Project: This BAdI allows you to validate values for properties of project elements such as WBS elements.
    • Project Element Determination - Enterprise Project: This can be used for determining values.

3. Code Example for Validation

Below is a conceptual example used to prevent project or work package release without budget allocation, which can be adapted for your requirement:

DATA(CURRENT_USER) = CL_ABAP_CONTEXT_INFO=>GET_USER_TECHNICAL_NAME().
SELECT SINGLE * FROM YY1_USERID INTO @DATA(LV_USER) WHERE USERDATA = @CURRENT_USER.
IF SY-SUBRC <> 0.
IF ENTERPRISEPROJECTELEMENT-PROCESSINGSTATUS = '10'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGECLASS = '/S4PPM/VAL_DET'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGETYPE = 'E'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGENUMBER = '000'.
LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE-SYSTEMMESSAGEVARIABLE1 = 'USER IS NOT AUTHORIZED TO RELEASE THE WORKPACKAGE'.
APPEND LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE TO VALIDATIONMESSAGES.
CLEAR LS_VALIDATIONMESSAGE.
ENDIF.
ENDIF.

4. Configure Custom Fields

  • Use the Custom Fields app to create the necessary fields to distinguish between project types or IDs within your authorization logic.

Considerations

  • Ensure that the code within the BAdIs only runs for specific events or processing statuses that you are interested in.
  • Regularly test and debug custom logic in a non-production environment before deploying.

By using these custom validations, you can restrict WBS elements based on project scope effectively.

Help Resources

For further details, explore the following related resources: Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App

  • Custom Fields App and Custom Logic App
  • App Extensibility: WBS Element

This approach should help to enforce control over WBS element assignment based on your project’s specifics.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: How can I make appear the internal price in the servi...
entry.content:
Hello,

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, pricing procedures help determine the final pricing for service quotations and service orders, but these do not automatically show internal costs. When managing costs related to a service that isn’t relevant to stock valuation, these are typically not reflected in the price details directly within service quotations or orders.

Steps to Reflect Costs:

1. Manual Price Entry

  • Use manual condition types like PMP0 to enter costs directly Pricing and Billing of Service Quotations in service quotations or orders.

2. Custom Pricing Procedures

  • If internal costs are vital for your pricing strategy, you may need to adjust the pricing procedures to include a custom condition type that represents internal pricing or costs. This involves the creation of condition tables and access sequences to directly associate these costs within the pricing methods.

3. Use of Service Quotation Quotation Types

  • Standard Service Quotations (SVQ1) and Fixed Price Service Quotations (SVQ2) allow you to handle items by fixed price or consumption-based billing. Ensure all pricing conditions are set to include relevant costs.

4. Training Sales Representatives

  • Educate sales reps to understand the process of manually inputting or verifying costs in quotations and services orders.

5. Set Custom Conditions Types

  • Utilize the SAP help documentation to assign custom condition types that can pick up non-stock related costs or manual entries.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Upgrade 1709 to SAP 2023 On-Premise from front/back end to embedded catalog
entry.content:
Does anyone know how to export/download catalogs and tiles?  I just need the Z* ones.   I can't seem to find anything.   The front-end system has been converted to 2023.   But we want to embed the system.   To do this all the catalogs and tiles have to be moved.   I can't seem to find anything on it.   And it seems like it would be a common request.  So I hate to ask.  I'll keep looking as well.  But any help would be appreciated.

Thank you!

Michelle

entry.title: Re: I want to understand how cost travel from sales order...
entry.content:
Hello,

To understand how cost transfers between a sales order and a purchase order, particularly in a 2-tier ERP system involving SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the scenario can be broken down into several steps.

Key Steps in the Cost Transfer Process

  1. Initialization: The subsidiary creates a purchase order in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. This order represents a request for goods or services.

  2. Using APIs: The purchase orders created in SAP S/4HANA Cloud are read using the API Purchase Order to Sales Order Creation in a 2-Tier… - SAP Community available at api.sap.com.

  3. Mapping to Sales Order IDOC: The purchase orders are mapped to a Sales Order IDOC (Intermediate Document) format suitable for transmission to an on-premise system at the headquarters.

  4. IDOC Transmission: The mapped IDOCs are then sent through the SAP Cloud Connector to the headquarters’ on-premise system.

  5. Processing at Headquarters: The headquarters processes the received IDOCs and converts them into sales orders.

  6. Cost Allocation: The cost associated with the purchase order is captured as the purchase turns into a sales order. This allows seamless integration of cost from procurement to sales, ensuring that all financials are in order as the transaction progresses from purchase to fulfillment.

Configuration

  • Communication arrangements between cloud and on-premise systems need to be set up to facilitate this workflow.
  • Partner Profiles and programming interfaces must be correctly configured at headquarters.

Additional Resources

  • For a detailed configuration guide on how communication between the SAP S/4HANA Cloud and on-premise systems for this purpose is set, you can refer to specific documentation available on SAP’s official site.

This process ensures that costs and other financial entries are accurately transferred and reconciled between the systems, leveraging both cloud capabilities and traditional on-premise infrastructure.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Release Standard Price in Valuation Typ 01 (Own Produ...
entry.content:
Hello,

When using split valuation and the standard price calculation, it’s crucial to ensure that the standard price is updated correctly across different valuation types. Here are the steps and considerations:

Steps to Release Standard Price to Valuation Type 1

  1. Ensure Proper Configuration:

    • In the material master record, double-check the settings for the valuation category and valuation type. Ensure that the valuation category ‘B’ (for procurement type) is correctly configured.

  2. Run a Cost Estimate:

    • Execute a standard cost estimate for the material. Ensure that this cost estimate is without errors.

  3. Mark and Release Standard Cost Estimate:

    • Use the app ‘Release Material Cost Estimates’ to mark and release Inventory Accounting - Marking and Releasing Standard Cost Estimates the standard cost estimate. When releasing, verify that the calculations reflect the required valuation type.

  4. Check Configuration for Split Valuation:

    • Verify in the configuration activities for split valuation that the standard price updates the valuation type accordingly. The valuation category Supply Chain - Valuation Category for split valuation must be adequately set to differentiate partial stocks.

  5. System Capabilities:

    • The SAP system should be set up to manage these ledger-specific material prices correctly by configuration in Manage Material Valuations or using specific APIs.

  6. Verify Period of Validity:

    • When releasing the standard cost estimate, ensure the validity period matches the current period in the material master to update correctly.

Troubleshooting

  • Check if Goods Receipt Based Invoice Verification is Defined: Access the purchase order, and ensure that goods receipt-based invoice verification is defined, allowing the system to handle partial deliveries separately.

  • Assess if Background Processing Matches Fiscal Settings: Make sure any existing background jobs are scheduled correctly, matching fiscal period requirements if you have variance in fiscal years.

Summary

Following these steps should help in updating the standard price for specific valuation types. It’s important to follow these considerations during the costing and release process to ensure the updates reflect accurately across all required valuation types.

For further details, you can refer to the documentation on marking and releasing standard cost estimates and split valuation Supply Chain - Creating Material Subject to Split Valuation configuration.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Questionmark cannot launch sample exam / I s this the only way?
entry.content:
Hi @Natalia1973 
Thank you for your engagement and posting this very important question. 
It looks like this is a new question.
I would like to encourage to used our SAP Learning Q&A section  and post new questions there.
This makes it easier  to follow-up:
That way other learners can benefit from the answer:
If you ask the question there other learners  can search for it in the future if they run into the same issue and there we have more features available for responses.
Would you mind to repost it and also provide more details regarding the issue you are facing.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: 免费销售订单，交货单过账时进费用科目
entry.content:
Hello,

To configure movement types for sales order schedule lines to ensure that delivery notes post to different accounts, you can follow these steps:

  1. Identify Movement Types: In SAP, movement types are three-digit identifiers that define the purpose of a goods movement, Supply Chain - The Movement Type Concept like a goods receipt or a goods issue. The movement type determines the accounting effects, such as which accounts are debited or credited.

  2. Link Movement Types to Schedule Lines: Ensure that the movement types are properly linked to the sales order schedule lines. This can be achieved by setting rules or configurations that determine the movement type for each schedule line based on specific criteria, like the nature of the transaction (e.g., customer relations vs. customer complaints).

  3. Configure Account Determination: Movement types can be associated with specific accounts by configuring account determination in SAP. This involves specifying the rules for which account each movement type posts to, based on the context of the transaction.

  4. Testing and Validation: Once configured, ensure to test the system by simulating transactions to validate that the correct accounts are used for different types of deliveries.

Example Implementation

In your specific scenario:

  • Customer Relations: Use a movement type configured to post to the sales expense account.
  • Customer Complaints: Use a different movement type that posts to the main business cost account.

These configurations allow automation in the attribution of costs to the correct accounts based on the type of sale or delivery involved.

SAP App for Movement Types

The SAP ‘Post Goods Movement’ app helps manage different types of goods movements, SAP Movement Types in SAP Cloud Public Edition including goods issues and receipts based on the movement type defined.

 

By utilizing the movement type concept efficiently, you can ensure accurate financial reporting and account management in your SAP system.

要配置销售订单计划行的移动类型，以确保送货单记入不同的账户，您可以按照以下步骤进行：

识别移动类型：在SAP中，移动类型是定义货物移动目的的三位数标识符，例如货物接收或货物发出。移动类型决定了会计影响，例如哪些账户被借记或贷记。
将移动类型链接到计划行：确保移动类型正确链接到销售订单计划行。这可以通过设置规则或配置来实现，根据特定标准（例如交易性质，如客户关系与客户投诉）确定每个计划行的移动类型。
配置账户确定：可以通过在SAP中配置账户确定来将移动类型与特定账户关联。这涉及到指定每个移动类型根据交易背景记入哪个账户的规则。
测试和验证：配置完成后，确保通过模拟交易测试系统，以验证不同类型的交付使用正确的账户。
具体场景实施示例：

客户关系：使用配置为记入销售费用账户的移动类型。
客户投诉：使用不同的移动类型记入主要业务成本账户。
这些配置允许根据销售或交付类型自动将成本归属到正确的账户。

SAP移动类型应用程序
SAP“货物移动过账”应用程序帮助管理不同类型的货物移动，包括基于定义的移动类型的货物发出和接收。

货物移动过账应用程序

通过有效利用移动类型概念，您可以确保SAP系统中的财务报告和账户管理的准确性。

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Hide People selector button in Goals. release 2025
entry.content:
Hello everyone, who is asking about translations for the Hide/Show People Selector, i found an answer in this KBA:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3607019

 

It says: "Note: These enhancements are patched to the 1H 2025 release, so translations of the labels 'Show/ Hide People Selector' won't be available until the 2H 2025 release. Moreover, the language key for these labels won't be available until the 2H 2025 release, so at this time there is no workaround to customize the labels. "
